observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Accountant record and  report in the financial statements of its public utilities or economic enterprises all cost of improvements, repairs and other related expenses incurred in the operation to present and reflect the actual performance of its operation.

We recommend that management effect the proper liquidation of all implemented and completed projects and return the remaining balances of the funds to the source agencies.  For the utilization of excess funds for purposes other than the specific purpose of the fund, authority from the source agencies shall be obtained before its utilization to avoid suspension/disallowance in audit.

We recommend that management request for Authority from the Commission on Audit to write-off receivable accounts determined to be uncollectible and doubtful, pursuant to Sec. 36 of PD 1445.  The request shall be supported with documents enumerated in Section 8.3 of COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016 following the procedures provided in Section 8 (Procedures on the write-off of dormant accounts) of the same COA Circular.

We recommend management to strictly impose the penalties and sanctions provided in Sections 16 and 20 of Municipal Ordinance No. 026 Series of 2014 for late and non-payment of water bills.  We also recommend the Municipal Treasurer to enforce collections of the delinquent accounts to increase the revenue of the waterworks system to at least lessen the losses in its operation.

1.6 We recommended that management require the Inventory Committee to prioritize and ensure full implementation of the “One-time cleansing of PPE accounts” in accordance with COA Circular No. 2020-006.

2.6 We recommended that the Inventory Committee conduct a physical count of inventories and submit the Report of Physical Count of Inventories (RPCI) within the prescribed period. We also recommend that the GSO maintain a stock card for each type of inventories and prepare a weekly Summary of Supplies Issued (SSMI).

3.7 We recommended that the Municipal Accountant to record purchases of supplies and materials in the agency's books using the proper inventory account as provided in the New Government Accounting System Manual for LGUs, Volume I. Thereafter, the Municipal Accountant shall record the issuance of supplies to end users in the appropriate expense account based on the SSMI duly supported by the approved RIS at least every month.
4.5 We recommended and management agreed that the Municipal Accountant disclose the amount and details of the unexpended balance of the LDRRMF to the Notes to the Financial Statements.
5.5 We recommended that management shift from a manual accounting system to the e-NGAS accounting system which offers various advantages.

6.6 We recommended that management stop utilizing the 5% LDRRMF for projects and activities other than those enumerated in Section 5.0 of NDRRMC-DBM-DILG JMC No. 2013-1, which are directly related to disaster prevention and mitigation, preparedness, response, and rehabilitation and recovery to maximize the attainment of the purpose of the fund.

7.6 We recommended and the management agreed that the concerned officials and employees submit all the necessary documentary requirements in the payment of Hazard Pay from August to December 2023 as provided in COA Circular 2012-01. The Municipal Accountant also agreed to ensure completeness of supporting documents before processing the payment of Hazard Pay.

8.8 We recommended and management agreed to adhere to the sufficient documentary requirement in the claims of RATA such as Certification by the HRMO or the Daily Time Record (DTR) for the regular employees under the Executive Department while Certification by the Municipal SB Secretary for the local sanggunian so that the allowances received for any given month are proper and based on the actual days worked.

9.5 We recommended and management agreed to require the suppliers to issue Official Receipts or Sales Invoices in the procurement of goods and services and adhere to the provision of COA Circular 2023-004 dated June 14, 2023.

10.6 We recommended and management agreed that the LDRRM Officer promptly submit the Monthly Report of Sources and Utilization of DRRMF, duly certified correct by the Municipal Accountant, on or before the 15th day after the end of each month.

We recommend that management exert more effort to enforce massive and strategic collections of delinquent real property taxes by frequently conducting tax campaigns and offering incentives to full payment of tax dues; or impose the available remedies provided for under the Local Government Code.

We also recommend the Municipal Assessor to exert more effort in updating the taxpayers of their revised or updated property tax dues through timely giving of the Notice of Assessments. Likewise input to the system an up-to-date Assessment Roll in order to reflect an updated assessment of real property tax collectibles of the municipality.

Furthermore, management need to revisit the Staffing Pattern recommended for the Office of the Municipal Assessor provided in the Manual on Real Property Appraisal and Assessment Operations should there be a need to increase manpower after thorough deliberation.

It is recommended that Management has to see to it that disbursements have to be supported with complete documentary requirements pursuant to Section 4(6) of PD 1445 and explain the above-noted deficiencies.

We recommend that management revisit the current regular plantilla items of the LGU and proposed/create another plantilla position when necessary to avoid hiring of contract of service and job order workers to perform the duties and responsibilities of a regular employee.  Accordingly, qualified COS/JOs shall be hired to fill in the newly created plantilla positions giving priority to those who have rendered more than 3 years in service to the municipality.
Instruct the Municipal Planning Development Officer to facilitate the implementation of projects without delay to preclude escalation of prices and to make services promptly available to the public.

We recommend that management instruct the Municipal Accountant to record the purchases of accountable forms or its appropriate inventory account pursuant to Section 51 of the Manual on the New Government Accounting System for LGUs.

For the unutilized inventories at year end, instruct the Municipal Treasurer/ GSO to conduct a physical inventory and the Municipal Accountant to book the same as inventory in the books of the municipality.

We recommend that the Municipal Agriculturist exert effort to verify the composition of Biological Assets account and regularly monitor its changes to facilitate the proper valuation of these assets and in order to reflect the accuraWte value of the account. Monitoring records and reports have to be provided also to the Accounting Office for proper recording in the books to fairly present the financial statements of the LGU.

We recommend that the Municipal Agriculturist exert effort to verify the composition of Biological Assets account and regularly monitor its changes to facilitate the proper valuation of these assets and in order to reflect the accurate value of the account. Monitoring records and reports have to be provided also to the Accounting Office for proper recording in the books to fairly present the financial statements of the LGU.
observations.managementAction: None
observations.reason: As of Dec. 31, 2021, Biological Assets account balance remained the same. No inventory/ physical count report was submitted to this office to account the value of the reported biological assets.

observations.recommendation: We recommended Management to provide copies of the certification on the full completion of the structures to the Municipal Accountant to be the basis in the recording of all completed projects in the CIP accounts to the appropriate PPE accounts and prepare the necessary journal entries in the General and Trust Fund books for proper recording and accounting of the completed infrastructure projects.

We recommended the Municipal Accountant to undertake a thorough review and analysis of the details of the accounts payable and effect the necessary adjustments to revert long outstanding and invalid claims to the unappropriated surplus of the general fund.  We also recommend the Municipal Accountant to refrain from recognizing obligations and recording of Accounts Payable for those claims not properly and completely documented to present fairly the expenses and payable accounts in the financial statements of the LGU.

We recommend that management instruct the Municipal Accountant to record the purchases of accountable forms or its appropriate inventory account pursuant to Section 51 of the Manual on the New Government Accounting System for LGUs.
For the unutilized inventories at year end, instruct the Municipal Treasurer/ GSO to conduct a physical inventory and the Municipal Accountant to book the same as inventory in the books of the municipality.

We recommend that management require the Inventory Committee to conduct complete physical count of the property, plant and equipment (PPE) and inventories, and submit copies of the Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE) and Report on the Physical Count of Inventories (RPCI) to the Municipal Accountant for reconciliation of records and to the Auditor, not later than January 31 of each year, for its verification.

It is recommended that Management has to see to it that disbursements have to be supported with complete documentary requirements pursuant to Section 4(6) of PD 1445 and explain the above-noted deficiencies.

We recommend that management revisit the current regular plantilla items of the LGU and proposed/create another plantilla position when necessary to avoid hiring of contract of service and job order workers to perform the duties and responsibilities of a regular employee.  Accordingly, qualified COS/JOs shall be hired to fill in the newly created plantilla positions giving priority to those who have rendered more than 3 years in service to the municipality.
We recommend the Municipal Accountant to make the necessary adjustments in the books recognizing the BGCM grant as income as well as the expenses incurred from the fund.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant made the necessary adjustments thru JEV No. 2021-03-220B
observations.reason: Review of journals and financial statements.

observations.recommendation: We recommend the Municipal Accountant to make the necessary adjustments in the books recognizing the accumulated depreciation of the communication equipment under the SEF books
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant made the necessary adjustments thru JEV No. 2021-03-06B
observations.reason: Review of journals and financial statements.

observations.recommendation: We recommend to the Municipal Accountant to take up the receivable reciprocal account in the General Fund Book of the LGU to recognize the interest income earned from trust fund deposits.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant made the necessary adjustments thru JEV No. 2021-03-220E
observations.reason: Review of journals and financial statements.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management maintain a special account in the General Fund for the Bayanihan Grant to Cities and Municipalities. Separate financial statements should be prepared and presented as part of the General Fund for easy monitoring of the utilization and to ensure the availability of this fund to support COVID prevention programs and projects.
observations.managementAction: Management admitted its failure to create a special account for the BGCM fund in the General Fund, however, because the BGCM grant was almost expended resulting to a minimal balance of ₱256.00 as of year-end of CY2020, they decided not to create the special account in the GF.
observations.reason: Management agreed to implement our recommendation in the same future transactions.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) comply with the mandatory posting of the updated APP, NOA, and other information relative to the Emergency Procurement under the Bayanihan Act in the GPPB Online Portal as required in GPPB Circular No. 1-2020.   Henceforth, we recommend that the BAC should always be updated with the issuances and advisories of the GPPB by visiting their website regularly to avoid any more non-compliance in the future.
observations.managementAction: The BAC had posted the NOA, PO and NTP as required.
observations.reason: Verified printed copies of the postings.

observations.recommendation: We recommend the municipal accountant to explain the differences between the account balances as presented in the financial statements with the depreciation schedule for CY-2020. We further recommend to reconcile the differences and make the necessary adjustments in the books to fairly present the financial statements of the LGU as of December 31, 2020.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant made a comprehensive reconciliation of the differences between the account balances of accumulated depreciation reflected in the financial statements and depreciation schedule.  Necessary adjustments were made thru JEV No. 2021-03-220A and 2021-03-220D.
observations.reason: Review of journals, schedule of depreciation and trial balances.

observations.recommendation: We recommend the Municipal Accountant to make the necessary adjustments in the books of the municipality dropping the cost of the structures and transfer the same to the books of the recipient barangays. Turn-over documents of the barangay structures including newly completed structures shall be attached to the JEV transferring the cost of the structures.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant transferred the cost of completed barangay projects totaling ₱17,997,999.30 to the recipient barangays per JEV No. 2021-03-220F.
observations.reason: Review of the Schedule of Property, Plant and Equipment disclosed that there are still completed  barangay structures totaling ₱14,191,151.71 (Annex G) which remained  in the books of the municipality as of December 31, 2021.  These were completely transferred to the barangays on March 25, 2022.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management conduct immediate verification on the deficiencies occurred in the remittance of the taxes, premiums and loan amortizations of officials and employees to protect the finances of the local government unit. Further, require the municipal accountant to thoroughly check all remittances and trace back to the respective payrolls to ensure accurate figures or remittances and trace back to the respective payrolls to ensure accurate figures of remittance to the different government agencies to protect officials and employees who have been religiously paying their indebtedness.
observations.managementAction:
The Municipal Accountant explained that the monthly billing statements of the GSIS were not updated and does not immediately reflect the updates sent by the LGU, thus resulted in the differences of account balances between the LGU and GSIS books.  Likewise, differences in the Due to PAG-IBIG and PHIC accounts are due to non-updated records.

The Municipal Accountant further explained that updating of these agencies records would take several months before their system would reflect the changes, thus differences will not be immediately reconciled.

observations.recommendation: Instruct the Municipal Planning Development Officer to facilitate the implementation of projects without delay to preclude escalation of prices and to make services promptly available to the public.
observations.managementAction:
Management explained that implementation of trust fund funded projects were prioritized because of time constraint in the implementation of the intended projects. Furthermore, they
will revisit and review the identified projects under the 20% Development Fund and evaluate what projects to be prioritized.
observations.reason: As of December 31, 2021, 63% of the current and continuing appropriations of the 20% Development Fund were partially and not implemented.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Agriculturist exert effort to verify the composition of Biological Assets account and regularly monitor its changes to facilitate the proper valuation of these assets and in order to reflect the accurate value of the account. Monitoring records and reports have to be provided also to the Accounting Office for proper recording in the books to fairly present the financial statements of the LGU.

We recommend that management observe and comply with the provisions prescribed in Sec. 325 (h) of RA 7160. Actual expenses for EME in the ensuing year should be within the required limitation to avoid disallowance in audit.
observations.managementAction: The LGU did not appropriate for Extraordinary and Miscellaneous Expense for CY 2020.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that management: 
a. Install strong internal control system regarding employee punctuality to discourage habitual tardiness in the LGU.

b. To send letters to the personnel enumerated therein, reprimanding them, as first offense, of the violation committed and reminding about the second and third offenses they may possibly receive if not corrected.

c. Submit to this Office the said letters with date and signature of the recipients, as duly received by them.
observations.managementAction: The practice of giving 15 minutes allowance per lates has been stopped. Each of the enumerated personnel were sent reprimanding letters by management. Then copies of the letters, duly received by the recipients, were submitted to this office on March 20, 2020.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management require the property custodian of the municipality to have all motor vehicles owned by the municipal government, except security vehicle, marked “For Official Use Only”, under which shall be written the information required under Section B of COA Circular no. 77-61.
observations.managementAction: Motor Vehicles of the LGU were now taped/marked with “For Official Use Only” sign

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management would provide COA auditors an office space within the Municipal Hall premises pursuant to Section 20(1) of PD No. 1445.
observations.managementAction: The LGU has already provided the COA Auditors an office room within the municipal compound

observations.recommendation: We recommend the management to adhere to the provision of Section 4(6) of Presidential Decree (PD) No. 1445 that all claims/payments against the government fund should be completely supported with the necessary documents for the validity of the transactions paid to avoid suspensions/disallowance in audit.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant see to it that all transactions were completely documented.

observations.recommendation: In view of these circumstances, we recommend that management immediately increase the Fidelity Bonds of the DO and all accountable officers of the municipality to an amount enough to cover their accountabilities in accordance with the Revised Schedule of Premium Rates of Fidelity Bond issued by the Bureau of Treasury.
observations.managementAction: Increased the premium of the Disbursing Officer

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Treasurer shall stamp “PAID” the disbursement vouchers and all supporting documents after payment and indicate therein the check number of checks issued and the date of actual payment to prevent possible re-use or recycling of documents to safeguard government resources against loss or wastage.
observations.managementAction: Stamped "PAID" the Disbursement Vouchers and all supporting documents

Only regular employees should attend trainings/seminars to achieve long term benefits from the expenses incurred for trainings and seminars.

observations.managementAction: Recommendations implemented. Employees/ Officials are not allowed to take personal loans that would bring their net take home pay to below ₱4,000.00.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management provide a venue to discuss with the municipal employees the role of the LGU in ensuring that its personnel are able to receive a minimum of ₱4,000.00 take home pay per month in pursuance of Section 47 of the 2017 GAA.
observations.reason:
Net take home pay of government employees per month minimum threshold has been updated to ₱5,000.00, per GAA 2018, instead of ₱4,000.00.

Based from the latest three months Salary Payrolls (Aug. - Oct. 2019) submitted  to this office as of Feb. 26, 2020 , the following are the personnel whose net take home pay for a month falls below the ₱5,000.00 minimum requirement of GAA 2018:

1. N. Baluma (August to 
     October)
2. J. Amarille (October)
3. R. Nacorda (August and 
     October)
4. L. Echavia, Jr. (August 
     and October)
5. M. F. Almedilla (August 
     to October)
6. A. Anquilero (August)
7. C. Romerde (August)

We recommend that management:
a. Install strong internal control system regarding employee punctuality to discourage habitual tardiness in the LGU.
b. To send letters to the personnel enumerated therein, reprimanding them, as first offense, of the violation committed and reminding about the second and third offenses they may possibly receive if not corrected.

observations.recommendation: We recommend management to stop the practice of granting cash advances to job order/contract of service personnel pursuant to Section 2.3 of COA Circular No. 96-004. observations.managementAction: Stopped granting taxi expenses reimbursement without ORs. Only taxi expenses with OR's were reimbursed.
observations.reason: Post-audit of Disbursement/ Liquidation Vouchers

observations.recommendation: We recommend the management to follow the rates prescribed under Section 4 of Executive Order No. 298 in claiming travel allowance/per diem for official travels to avoid disallowance in audit.
observations.reason: Post-audit of sampled Disbursement/ Liquidation Vouchers disclosed that travel diems/ allowances given are now in consonance with the latest government travel allowance guidelines.

observations.recommendation: We recommend the management to adhere to the provision of Section 4(6) of Presidential Decree (PD) No. 1445 that all claims/payments against the government fund should be completely supported with the necessary documents for the validity of the transactions paid to avoid suspensions/disallowance in audit.
observations.managementAction: Travel reimbursements/liquidations involving air flights have Boarding Pass attachment and accommodation claims in excess of the travel allowance has Certifications from the Mayor.
observations.reason: Post-audit of Disbursement/ Liquidation Vouchers

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management evaluate the status if their job order employees and recommend those qualified for permanent positions. Furthermore, management needs to immediately revisit their vacant plantilla positions and consider the employees with job order status with long years of service for such vacant position.
observations.managementAction: For CY 2019, three (3) personnel were newly hired by the LGU for vacant plantilla position and these personnel were not the nine (9) personnel enumerated in the AOM.
observations.reason:
Based from DBM, CSC and COA Joint Circular No. 1 s2017, Contract of Service or Job Order Personnel shall be given priority in the appointment by the agency to its vacant positions. But provided that they meet the appropriate eligibility and other qualification requirements for the position.
 
Inquiry from the HRMO disclosed that most of the personnel enumerated does not have the eligibility requirement.

Even assuming that they have meet the appropriate eligibility and other qualification requirements for the position, management still has the ultimate decision of whether to accept the said personnel or not for the vacant positions for the Joint Circular  provides prioritization, not mandatory/ automatic selection.

observations.recommendation: It is recommended that the management submit an explanation for the non-creation of the LCPC. It is further recommended that management create a Local Council for the Protection of Children which could directly cater the needs of the supposed beneficiaries. Moreover, it is recommended that management appropriate 1% of its IRA for LCPC related programs, activities and projects.
observations.managementAction: LCPC budget were incorporated in the GAD budget. Also, on October 2019, LGU-Maribojoc had created the Municipal Council for the Protection of Children (MCPC).
observations.reason: Based from the submitted Maribojoc Executive Order No. 30, s2019 received by this office on February 26, 2020.

observations.reason: PPEs enumerated in this AOM were the same PPEs enumerated in the AOM found in AAR 2017, thus reiterated AOM/redundant.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Treasurer shall stamp “PAID” the disbursement vouchers and all supporting documents after payment and indicate therein the check number of checks issued and the date of actual payment to prevent possible re-use or recycling of documents to safeguard government resources against loss or wastage.
observations.managementAction: Only Disbursement Vouchers (DVs) and few of the supporting documents were stamped "PAID".
observations.reason: Post-audit of Disbursement and Liquidation Vouchers disclosed that majority of Official Receipts, Sales Invoices, Collection Receipts, Itineraries, tickets and other supporting documents were not stamped "PAID".

observations.recommendation: Require the Municipal Accountant to make the necessary adjustments in the books of the municipality dropping the cost of structures and transfer the same to the books of the recipient barangays. Turn-over documents of newly completed structures/ shall be attached to the JEV transferring the cost of the structures.
observations.managementAction: Turn-over documents were already prepared. JEV transferring these barangay structures are still to be prepared.
observations.reason: Review on the Schedule of Property, Plant and Equipments for CY 2018 showed various completed PPE projects undertaken by the municipality, however, these were not yet transferred to the recipient barangays.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management conduct immediate verification on the deficiencies occurred in the remittance of the taxes, premiums and loan amortizations of officials and employees to protect the finances of the local government unit. Further, require the municipal accountant to thoroughly check all remittances and trace back to the respective payrolls to ensure accurate figures or remittances and trace back to the respective payrolls to ensure accurate figures of remittance to the different government agencies to protect officials and employees who have been religiously paying their indebtedness.
observations.managementAction: Municipal Accountant on the process of reconciling the said accounts from different NGAS.
observations.reason:
Transition of the unreconciled amounts from CY 2018 to CY 2019, based from the Journals and Ledgers:

* Due to GSIS 
   = ₱142,019.58
* Due to PAG-IBIG   
   =₱17,454.81
* Due to PhilHealth
   = ₱5,788.65

observations.recommendation: Instruct the Municipal Planning Development Officer to facilitate implementation of projects without delay to preclude escalation of prices and to make services promptly available to the public.

We recommend that management evaluate the status if their job order employees and recommend those qualified for permanent positions. Furthermore, management needs to immediately revisit their vacant plantilla positions and consider the employees with job order status with long years of service for such vacant position.
observations.managementAction: AAR CY 2017 | One employee was appointed to a regular position, however, said person is not among those JOs continuously hired by the LGU.

observations.recommendation: It is recommended that the management submit an explanation for the non-creation of the LCPC. It is further recommended that management create a Local Council for the Protection of Children which could directly cater the needs of the supposed beneficiaries. Moreover, it is recommended that management appropriate 1% of its IRA for LCPC related programs, activities and projects.
observations.managementAction: AAR CY 2017 | The programs for LCPC were incorporated in the GAD Budget.

observations.recommendation: Require the Municipal Accountant to make the necessary adjustments in the books of the municipality dropping the cost of the structures and transfer the same to the books of the recipient barangays. Turn-over documents of newly completed structures shall be attached to the JEV transferring the cost of the structures.

We recommend that management explain non-implementation of various projects under the LDRRM Plans and slow pace of those implemented, thereby defeating the purpose for which the fund was established. Further, mobilize personnel to study and monitor whether feasible to be implemented, otherwise, select other projects that are viable and beneficial for the community.

We recommend that management explain/justify its failure to conduct of required physical inventory-taking and create a team that will conduct physical inventory-taking and create a team that will conduct physical inventory taking of all Municipality's properties.

We recommend that management require the Inventory Committee to conduct complete physical count of the property, plant and equipment (PPE) and inventories, and submit copies of the Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE) and Report on the Physical Count of Inventories (RPCI) to the Municipal Accountant for reconciliation of records and to the Auditor, not later than January 31 of each year, for its verification.

We recommend the GSO to prepare the Summary of Supplies and Materials Issued (SSMI) and submit copies of the summary to the Municipal Accountant which will be her basis in recording supplies and materials expenses to obtain accurate supplies and materials expense accounts.
Instruct the Municipal Planning Development Officer to facilitate the implementation of projects without delay to preclude escalation of prices and to make services promptly available to the public.

We recommend the Municipal Accountant to transfer the unutilized DRMM Fund in the trust fund books in compliance with Section 5.1.10 of COA Circular No. 2012-002 to present fairly the financial statements of the LGU and to ensure its availability and utilization in times of disasters and emergencies only.

We recommend the Municipal Accountant to make the necessary adjustments in the books in compliance with Section 5.1.11 of COA Circular No. 2012-002 to present fairly the financial statements of the LGU.

We recommend the Municipal Accountant to transfer the unspent LDRRM Funds to the General Fund books to make it available for other social services in compliance with Section 5.1.13 of COA Circular No. 2012-002.

We recommend the Municipal Accountant to observe appropriate use of accounts to present fairly the financial statements of the LGU. Registration fees from trainings/conventions and seminars/workshops should be recorded as Training expenses, rather than Traveling Expenses pursuant to the account description provided in COA Circular No. 2015-009.

We recommend that the Municipal Treasurer has to stamp “PAID” the disbursement vouchers and all supporting documents after payment and indicate therein the check number of checks issued and the date of actual payment to prevent possible re-use or recycling of documents to safeguard government resources against loss or wastage.

We recommend management to stop the practice of granting cash advances to job order/contract of service personnel pursuant to Section 2.3 of COA Circular No. 96-004. Only regular officials and employees have to be granted cash advances for travels to safeguard possible loss or wastage of government funds.

We recommend the management to stop the practice of allowing job order/contract of services personnel to attend seminars/trainings. Only regular employees should attend trainings/seminars to achieve long term benefits from the expenses incurred for trainings and seminars.
We recommend the Municipal Accountant to require Official Receipts (OR) for all claims of taxi fares regardless of the amount to avoid disallowance in audit.
We recommend the management to follow the rates prescribed under Section 4 of Executive Order No. 298 in claiming travel allowance/per diem for official travels to avoid disallowance in audit.

We recommend the LGU to use the Certificate of Expenses not Requiring Receipts (CENRR) as supporting document for all small value claims below ₱300.00 as prescribed under COA Circular No. 2017-001 to avoid  disallowance in audit.

We recommend the Municipal Accountant to require the  use of the Certificate of Expenses not Requiring Receipts (CENRR) form, as prescribed under COA Circular No. 2017-001, instead of Reimbursement Expenses Receipt (RER) form to avoid suspensions in audit.

We recommend the management to adhere to the provision of Section 4(6) of Presidential Decree (PD) No. 1445 that all claims/payments against the government fund should be completely supported with the necessary documents for the validity of the transactions paid to avoid suspensions/disallowance in audit.

Direct the Municipal Accountant to make the necessary adjustments in the books for those assets which are not yet completed as of December 31, 2017 using the above prescribed journal entries. Henceforth, observe proper recording and use of accounts to present fairly the financial statements of the municipality.
observations.managementAction: AAR CY 2017 | The Municipal Accountant had adjusted the books in compliance with the recommendation of the prior year's recommendation and used the Construction in Progress accounts in recording started but not yet completed projects.

observations.recommendation: It is recommended that management submit the lacking/unsubmitted documents to prevent the above transactions from being suspended/disallowed in audit., Henceforth, the management must adhere to the provisions of Section 4(6) of PD No. 1445 that all claims/payments against the government fund should be completely supported with the necessary documents for the validity of the transactions paid.
observations.managementAction: AAR CY 2017 | Management followed the recommendation of prior year's observation.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management properly and completely fill-up Purchase Order and indicate therein the basic data as specified under COA Circular No. 96-010 dated August 15, 1996.
observations.managementAction: AAR CY 2017 | Management followed the recommendation of prior year's observation.

observations.recommendation: Direct the Municipal Treasurer to see to it that only permanent and duly bonded employees be designated to perform collecting functions pursuant to Sec. 101 of PD No. 1445 and Sec. 65, Volume I of the GAAM. Further reassign permanent employees from other departments during peak period, such as in January, to reinforce/augment the duly bonded collectors at the Treasurer's Office in serving the big volume of tax payers.
observations.managementAction: AAR CY 2017 | Collecting functions were now handled by bonded regular employee of the municipality.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management assign the functions of the Liquidating Officer to another regular employee of MTO-Maribojoc. In doing so, disbursing function is segregated from the collection functions.
observations.managementAction: AAR CY 2017 | Management followed the recommendation of prior year's observation. One employee now handles the liquidating functions while another employee handles disbursing functions.

observations.recommendation: We recommend management to return to the trustor or the Bureau of Treasury balances of unexpended funds received and all financial assistance which remained unimplemented for more than three years pursuant to the above-cited provisions of COA Circular No. 94-013.
observations.managementAction: AAR CY 2016 | Management returned the outstanding balances of trust receipts from national and local government agencies to their   respective trustors.

observations.recommendation:

1. Require an explanation from the BAC on the splitting of Contracts/Purchase Orders that amounted at least P1,151,034.39
2. Require the BAC to stop the splitting of contracts/PO s and adhere to the provisions of Section 54.1 of the RIRR of RA 9184 to avoid disallowance in audit.

observations.recommendation: Stop the practice of indicating brand names in the Purchase Request and Quotation Forms and adhere to the provision of Section 18 of RA 9184 to avoid disallowance in audit in the future transactions of the LGU.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management provide a venue to discuss with the municipal employees the role of the LGU in ensuring that its personnel are able to receive a minimum of P4,000.00 take home pay per month in pursuance of Section 47 of the 2017 GAA.
observations.managementAction: AAR CY 2017 | The number of personnel whose net take home pay is below P4,000.00 per month now decreased to two (2).

Turn-over documents of newly completed structure/s shall be attached to the JEV transferring the cost of the structure/s.

observations.managementAction: The Management explained that there is still no available stockroom for the bulk procurement of supplies, hence, were procured as needed by the concerned department.

observations.recommendation:
Recommendation:

      Direct the Municipal Accountant to make the necessary  adjustments  in
the books for those assets which are not yet completed as  of  December  31,
2017 using  the  above  prescribed  journal  entries.   Henceforth,  observe
proper recording and  use  of  accounts  to  present  fairly  the  financial
statements of the municipality.

Recommendation:

      Require the Municipal Accountant to make the necessary adjustments  in
the books of the municipality  dropping  the cost  of  the  structures  and
transfer the same to  the  books  of  the  recipient  barangays.   Turn-over
documents of newly completed  structure/s  shall  be  attached  to  the  JEV
transferring the cost of the structure/s.

Recommendation:

      We recommend that management conduct  immediate  verification  on  the
deficiencies occurred in the remittance of  the  taxes,  premiums  and  loan
amortizations of officials and employees to  protect  the  finances  of  the
local  government  unit.   Further,  require  the  municipal  accountant  to
thoroughly check all remittances and trace back to the  respective  payrolls
to ensure  accurate  figures  of  remittance  to  the  different  government
agencies to protect  officials  and  employees  who  have  been  religiously
paying their indebtedness.

Recommendations:

      Instruct the Municipal  Planning  Development  Officer  to  facilitate
implementation of projects without delay to preclude  escalation  of  prices
and to make services promptly available to the public.

Recommendation:

              It    is    recommended    that    management    submit    the
lacking/unsubmitted documents to prevent the above transactions  from  being
suspended/disallowed in audit.  Henceforth, the management  must  adhere  to
the provisions of Section 4(6) of  PD  No.  1445  that  all  claims/payments
against  the  government  fund  should  be  completely  supported  with  the
necessary documents for the validity of the transactions paid.

Recommendation:

      We recommend that management evaluate the status of  their  job  order
employees  and  recommend   those   qualified   for   permanent   positions.
Furthermore, management needs to immediately revisit their vacant  plantilla
positions and consider the employees with  job  order  status  with  long
years of service for such vacant position.

Recommendation:

      We recommend that management properly and completely fill-up  Purchase
Order and indicate therein the basic data as specified  under  COA  Circular
No. 96-010 dated August 15, 1996.

Recommendation:

      Direct the Municipal Treasurer to see to it that  only  permanent  and
duly  bonded  employees  be  designated  to  perform  collecting   functions
pursuant to Sec. 101 of PD No. 1445 and Sec.  65,  Volume  I  of  the  GAAM.
Further reassign permanent employees  from  other  departments  during  peak
period, such as in January, to reinforce/augment the duly bonded  collectors
at the Treasurer’s Office in serving the big volume of taxpayers.

Recommendation:

      We recommend that management assign the functions of  the  Liquidating
Officer  to  another  regular  employee  of  MTO-Maribojoc.   In  doing  so,
disbursing function is segregated from the collection functions.

Recommendation:


            Stop the practice of indicating  brand  names  in  the  Purchase
Request and Quotation Forms and adhere to the provision of Section 18 of  RA
9184 to avoid disallowance in audit in the future transactions of the LGU.

Require the Municipal Accountant to adhere to the proper and correct classification of accounts.  Use of journal entries as well as preparation of the financial statements shall always be in accordance with the Chart of Accounts as prescribed in COA Circular No. 2015-009, to be able to maintain accurate and reliable accounting records, and to ensure that the financial statements of the municipality are presented fairly.  Consequently, require the adjustment of the above enumerated accounts to its appropriate account titles.

Require the Municipal Accountant to adhere to the proper and correct classification of accounts.  Consequently, require the adjustment of the above enumerated accounts to their appropriate account titles as follows: (3-01-01-020) 480,156.79 (1-07-05-020) 169,529.69 (1-07-05-110) 405.00 (1-07-07-010) 144,237.10 (1-07-99-990) 165,985.00 #

Require the Municipal Accountant to established RPT and SET Receivables for the current year at the beginning of the year based on the report of the Municipal Treasurer showing the list of the taxpayers and the amount due to accurately present the RPT/SET Receivable accounts in the financial statements and to adhere to the afore-stated provisions of the NGAS Manual in the setting up of RPT/SET Receivables for the year.  Proper recording of RPT/SET collections should also be observed to present an accurate financial statement for the year.

We recommend management to return to the trustor or the Bureau of Treasury balances of unexpended funds received and all financial assistance which remained unimplemented for more than three years pursuant to the above-cited provisions of COA Circular No. 94-013.
observations.reason: As of December 31, 2017, long outstanding trust liabilities decreased to ₱1,668,835.40.

observations.recommendation: We recommend management to make the necessary adjustment in the books using the appropriate liability accounts to fairly present the financial statements of the municipality.

In view of the Supreme Court’s decision pronouncing the unconstitutionality of the PDAF, we recommend that the remaining funds shall no longer be released/used but shall be returned to the Bureau of Treasury so that it can be used for other priority programs of the government.

Require the Municipal Accountant to make the necessary adjustment in the books using the following journal enter in the ensuing year: (3-01-01-010) xxx (1-07-05-990) xxx # Further, the management shall prepare turn-over documents for each recipient barangays of the water dispenser and cancel all AREs previously issued to barangays.

We recommend management to: 1. Direct the Municipal Budget Officer to adhere to the provisions of Section 336 of RA 7160 in certifying as to the existence of appropriation.  Proper charging of expenditures should be observed to avoid disallowance in audit. 2. Direct the Municipal Accountant to adhere to the proper and correct classification of accounts.  Consequently, require the adjustment of above enumerated accounts to their appropriate asset accounts. 3. For monitoring, control and accountability, an Acknowledgement Receipt for Equipment (ARE) shall be prepared by the Property Officer.

We recommend that management explain non-implementation of various projects under the LDRRM Plans and slow pace of those implemented, thereby defeating the purpose for which the fund was established.  Further, mobilize personnel to study and monitor whether projects are still feasible to be implemented, otherwise, select other projects that are viable and beneficial for the community.

Instruct the Municipal Accountant to require strict compliance in the recording of liabilities only upon delivery/rendition of goods/services and receipt of bills to prevent the existence of undocumented accounts.
Require the Municipal Accountant to review and verify all unpaid claims to determine which accounts are due for reversion, pursuant to Section 98, PD 1445.

Instruct the Municipal Accountant to take in the books expenditures and obligations during the year they are incurred to reflect the correct expenses in the financial statement of the year under audit and to avoid disallowance in audit.

We recommend management to explain the above noted deficiencies and require all the signatories of the Inspection and Acceptance Report (IAR) to ensure that the items listed on the IAR are the exact same items purchased and in accordance with the required specifications.  We further recommend that the appropriate boxes in the IAR be marked as inspected and identify whether they are completely or partially accepted whichever applicable.

We recommend that management advice the Local School Board to make representations with the Sangguniang Bayan for the possible appropriation of funds in the General Fund for expenses deemed necessary for the operations of the school but are not eligible for inclusion in the local school board budget. Henceforth, stop the practice of charging expenses not aligned with the specific purpose of the SEF and require the Local School Board to adhere to the provisions of Section 272 of RA 7160 and Section 83 Vol. I of the Manual on the New Government Accounting System for LGUs.
observations.managementAction: None

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management make an effort to secure documents that would determine the cost of the donated assets, otherwise, require from the appraisal committee to appraise the donated equipment so that the Municipal Accountant can record the same in the books using the appraised value.
observations.managementAction: The donated floating tillers were not recorded in books because these were donated to farmer’s association in the municipality.

observations.recommendation: We recommend Management to require the personnel in the Inspection and Acceptance on all items of deliveries amounting to ₱9,83,128.12 to submit a sworned certificate of the actual date of delivery and henceforth date has to be indicated and filled up and supply all desired information in the required form. Further, we recommend Management to comply with established laws and regulations in documentation requirements before effecting payment thereof pursuant to Section 4(6) of PD 1445.

We recommend that management explain/justify its failure to conduct the required physical inventory-taking and create a team that will conduct physical inventory-taking of all the Municipality’s properties.
observations.managementAction: On-going preparation of the RPCPPE
observations.reason: RPCPPE as of December 31, 2017 is still for submission to this office. As of December 31, 2017, total PPE amounted to ₱137,551,870.78.

Require the Municipal Accountant to adhere to the proper and correct
classification of accounts. Use of journal entries as well as preparation of the
financial statements shall always be in accordance with the Chart of Accounts as
prescribed in COA Circular No. 2015-009, to be able to maintain accurate and
reliable accounting records, and to ensure that the financial statements of the
municipality are presented fairly. Consequently, require the adjustment of the
above enumerated accounts to its appropriate account titles.

Require the Municipal Accountant to make the necessary adjustments in the
books of the municipality dropping the cost of the structures and transfer the same
to the books of the recipient barangays. Turn-over documents of newly completed
structure/s shall be attached to the JEV transferring the cost of the structure/s.

Require the Municipal Accountant to adhere to the proper and correct
classification of accounts. Consequently, require the adjustment of the above

for the current year at the beginning of the year based on the report of the

accurately present the RPT/SET Receivable accounts in the financial statements and
to adhere to the afore-stated provisions of the NGAS Manual in the setting up of
RPT/SET Receivables for the year. Proper recording of RPT/SET collections

We recommend management to return to the trustor or the Bureau of
Treasury balances of unexpended funds received and all financial assistance which
remained unimplemented for more than three years pursuant to the above-cited
provisions of COA Circular No. 94-013.

We recommend management to make the necessary adjustment in the books
using the appropriate liability accounts to fairly present the financial statements of
the municipality.
Accounting of the LDRRM fund shall be pursuant to the prescribed
accounting entries as illustrated in COA Circular No. 2012-002.

In view of the Supreme Court’s decision pronouncing the unconstitutionality
of the PDAF, we recommend that the remaining funds shall no longer be
released/used but shall be returned to the Bureau of Treasury so that it can be used
for other priority programs of the government.

books using the following journal enter in the ensuing year:

Further, the management shall prepare turn-over documents for each
recipient barangays of the water dispenser and cancel all AREs previously issued to
barangays.

We recommend management to:
1. Direct the Municipal Budget Officer to adhere to the provisions of Section 336 of
RA 7160 in certifying as to the existence of appropriation. Proper charging of
expenditures should be observed to avoid disallowance in audit.
2. Direct the Municipal Accountant to adhere to the proper and correct
classification of accounts. Consequently, require the adjustment of above
enumerated accounts to their appropriate asset accounts.
3. For monitoring, control and accountability, an Acknowledgement Receipt for
Equipment (ARE) shall be prepared by the Property Officer.

We recommend that management explain non-implementation of various
projects under the LDRRM Plans and slow pace of those implemented, thereby
defeating the purpose for which the fund was established. Further, mobilize
personnel to study and monitor whether projects are still feasible to be
implemented, otherwise, select other projects that are viable and beneficial for the
community.

Instruct the Municipal Accountant to require strict compliance in the
recording of liabilities only upon delivery/rendition of goods/services and receipt of
bills to prevent the existence of undocumented accounts.

Require the Municipal Accountant to review and verify all unpaid claims to
determine which accounts are due for reversion, pursuant to Section 98, PD 1445.

Cause the taking up in the books expenditures and obligations during the
year they are incurred to reflect the correct expenses in the financial statement of
the year under audit and to avoid disallowance in audit.

We recommend management to explain the above noted deficiencies and
require all the signatories of the Inspection and Acceptance Report (IAR) to ensure
that the items listed on the IAR are the exact same items purchased and in
accordance with the required specifications. We further recommend that the
appropriate boxes in the IAR be marked as inspected and identify whether they are
completely or partially accepted whichever applicable.

We recommend that management adhere to the provisions of the NGAS Manual in accounting for supplies/materials for stocks and to observe appropriate use of accounts to obtain the accurate inventory account balances.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that rules and regulations provided under RA 9184 be strictly observed and implemented for transparency and competitiveness in government procurement system to avoid future suspensions/disallowances.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management observe the provisions of Section 3 of RA9184 to ensure and observe and ensure transparency and competitiveness in the procurement activity. We further recommend that the Bids and Awards Committee will provide wide dissemination of bid opportunities, send invitation to independent observers, extend equal opportunity and issued bid documents to all qualified parties to avoid presumption of rigged biddings and promote transparency and competitiveness in the procurement process.

Further, we recommend Management to comply with established laws and regulations in documentation requirements before effecting payment thereof pursuant to Section 4(6) of PD 1445.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the management require coordination between the Municipal Accountant/General Services Officer towards the implementation of the perpetual inventory method in recording supplies and materials to ensure proper accounting and promote better control in the receipt and issuance of supplies and materials.
